Enforcement 
322-6«1 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6,(44 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
Very truly yours,

Administration 
•• 
Executive/Legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
322·5901 
322-6«1 
March 1, 1985 
Robert S. Bower 
City Attorney of San Fernando 
Rutan & Tucker 
P.O. Box 1950 
Costa Mesa, CA 
92626 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-85-029 
Dear Mr. Bower: 
We are in receipt of your letter on behalf of the City of 
San Fernando seeking advice as to the possible requirement for 
disqualification of one of its councilmembers, who is not named 
in the letter. 
Under Government Code Section 83114(b) and 
Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329, we are 
unable to provide the City as a third party, with advice about 
the duties or obligations of one of its councilmembers. 
If the 
councilmember wishes to request our advice, she may do so either 
directly, through private counsel, or through your office (so 
long as it is specifically authorized by her). 
However, since 
we are not rendering advice, this letter provides no immunity 
from possible enforcement proceedings and she proceeds at her 
own risk in acting upon advice of private counsel. 
We have reviewed your legal memorandum dated November 18, 
1983, and find the legal analysis to be sound. 
We enclose 
copies of several advice letters which you may find helpful. 
Should you have any questions regarding this letter, I may be 
reached at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
.

sections.conclusion: Under Government Code Section 83114(b) and Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329, the FPPC is unable to provide the City as a third party with advice about the duties or obligations of one of its councilmembers. The councilmember must request advice directly, through private counsel, or through the City Attorney with specific authorization.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the City of San Fernando can receive FPPC advice regarding the possible disqualification requirements for one of its councilmembers under Government Code Section 83114(b).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide advice to a city regarding a councilmember's disqualification obligations on a third-party basis; the councilmember must request such advice directly or through authorized counsel, though the FPPC notes the city's legal memorandum analysis was sound.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two letters: initial referral (Feb 15, 1985) and substantive response (March 1, 1985). No formal QUESTION section header, but the substance of the request is clear from the body. Declination of advice on third-party basis with partial affirmation of requestor's legal analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the City of San Fernando can receive FPPC advice regarding the possible disqualification requirements for one of its councilmembers under Government Code Section 83114(b).

CONCLUSION: Under Government Code Section 83114(b) and Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329, the FPPC is unable to provide the City as a third party with advice about the duties or obligations of one of its councilmembers. The councilmember must request advice directly, through private counsel, or through the City Attorney with specific authorization.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice to the City of San Fernando regarding a councilmember's possible disqualification obligations, as this would constitute impermissible third-party advice, but confirmed the city's legal analysis was sound.
